content.full_text:
State of California                A-77-[illegible]                    87300
                    Fair Political Practices Commission
                    P O BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
                    Technical Assistance Administration • • • Enfor cer Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
                    (916) 322-5660                              322 5631              322 6441              322 6444


                                                                December 29, 1977


Mr. B. Richard Marsh
Knapp, Stevens, Grossman, and Marsh
1800 United California Bank Building
707 Wilshire Boulevard
Los Angeles, California  90017

    Re:  Southern California Coastal Water
         Research Project Authority

Dear Mr. Marsh:

    This is in response to your letter to the Commission of December 13,
1977, concerning the development of a Conflict of Interest Code for the
referenced joint powers agency. The staff is in the process of notifying
joint powers agencies for which we are the code reviewing body to submit
Codes not later than April 1, 1978. In addition to such notification, we
are including several instructional materials to aid in Code development
and adoption.

    I am also enclosing a copy of the staff's advice in the Encina Joint
Sewerage System matter. I doubt it applies to the referenced agency, but
I would invite your comments on its possible applicability.

    If I can be of any assistance in developing a Code of the agency,
please feel free to be in touch with me at (916)322-6444.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Research Specialist III
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:mfa
enclosure

[illegible text at top left]

1730

Fair Political Process, Inc.
1100 Ray Street
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: The Southern California Coastal Water Research
    Project Authority, a Joint Powers Agency

Gentlemen:

On two previous occasions we have written to you to inquire whether
or not the Commission had established a deadline for the adoption
of a Conflict of Interest Code that would be applicable to our above
referenced client.

In your bulletin dated November 13, 1972, you commented on Opinion
No. 70-85. You were then inclined to advise us that the Joint Joint Sewerage System
and the Joint Advisory Committee of the Inland Joint Sewerage System
are governmental agencies which are required to adopt Conflict of
Interest Codes. You have indicated, based on the Opinion on the basis of
a previously approved staff advice that neither entity is a governmental agency required to adopt such a Code.

It would be appreciated if you would advise me of the basis for the
opinion so that we might advise whether the other or not it is also applicable to the Southern California Coastal Water Research Project
Authority.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

B. RICHARD MARSH

BRM/la

cc:  Southern California Coastal Water Research
     Project Authority

WIXON STEVENS
WILLIAM H. CROSMAN
B. RICHARD MARSH
THOMAS A. DORAN
JAMES C. JONES
DAVID P. CHRISTIANSON
DANIEL V. HYDE

LAW OFFICES

KNAPP, STEVENS, GROSSMAN & MARSH
1800 UNITED CALIFORNIA BANK BUILDING
707 WILSHIRE BOULEVARD
LOS ANGELES 90017
TELEPHONE (213) 627-8471

WYMAN C. KNAPP
OF COUNSEL
JOSEPH C. GILL
1912-1968

HUGH GORDON
1884-1974

December 13, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 Kay Street
Sacramento, California 95814

RE:  The Southern California Coastal Water Research
     Project Authority, a Joint Powers Agency

Gentlemen:

     On two previous occasions we have written to you to inquire whether
or not the Commission had established a deadline for the adoption
of a Conflict of Interest Code that would be applicable to our above
referenced client.

     In your bulletin dated November 18, 1977, you commented on Opinion
76078.  You were then asked whether the Encina Joint Sewerage System
and the Joint Advisory Committee of the Encina Sewerage System
are governmental agencies which are required to adopt Conflict of
Interest Codes.  You apparently dismissed the Opinion on the basis of
a previously approved staff advice that neither entity is a governmental agency required to adopt such a Code.

     It would be appreciated if you would advise me of the basis for the
Opinion so that we might determine whether or not it is also applicable to the Southern California Coastal Water Research Project
Authority.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [Signature]
                                        B. RICHARD MARSH

BRM/la

cc:  Southern California Coastal Water Research
     Project Authority

sections.question_synthetic: The attorney is asking whether the Southern California Coastal Water Research Project Authority, a Joint Powers Agency, is required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code under FPPC regulations, and seeking clarification on how a previous opinion regarding the Encina Joint Sewerage System applies to their client.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry letter, not an FPPC response. No verbatim question or conclusion sections are present. The document references previous correspondence and a prior FPPC opinion (76078) regarding a related entity. Document quality is poor with some text degradation, but core content is identifiable.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney representing the Southern California Coastal Water Research Project Authority seeking FPPC guidance on whether their client is a governmental agency required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code, and requesting clarification on the basis for a prior dismissal of a similar opinion.
